user_prompt: modify `hfp_incoming_audio` and `hfp_outgoing_audio` to use libsbc when appropriate (when the stream needs it), assume that libsbc is infinitely fast, initialize the `sbc_struct` when required
agent_reply: Updated [src/bt_audio_demo.cpp](c:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/src/bt_audio_demo.cpp) so `hfp_incoming_audio()` and `hfp_outgoing_audio()` use `libsbc` when the active HFP codec mode is mSBC.
- Frees SBC state when switching to CVSD or disconnecting.
- CVSD remains direct passthrough at 8 kHz.
- outgoing: PDM/I2S PCM -> `sbc_encode()` -> HFP callback buffer

One caveat I left in the source comment: this library exposes generic SBC settings, not an explicit HFP mSBC/H2 transport mode. The codec use is isolated so we can adjust framing if the actual Bluetooth payload includes HFP-specific mSBC headers.

user_prompt: remove the libsbc from platformio.ini, as I have made a copy in `lib\sbc`, no code changes so far but verify what I did is valid
agent_reply: Your `lib\sbc` copy is valid for PlatformIO as-is: the build now detects it as a local project library named `sbc`, compiles it, and `#include "sbc.h"` resolves correctly.
One small note: PlatformIO is compiling the copied command-line/test files too:
They appear to compile into tiny object files and don’t break anything, but they aren’t needed for firmware. Later we may want a `library.json` or source filter to include only the codec files.

user_prompt: I made changes in `lib\AudioManager` related to the new switch `USE_BLUEDROID_MSBC` which switches between using bluedroid and libsbc
review these changes, implement the marked TODO items
agent_reply: - `AudioManager` now includes [conf.h](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/inc/conf.h) so `USE_BLUEDROID_MSBC` is actually visible there.
- Added `-I lib/bluedroid` to [platformio.ini](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/platformio.ini:12), since `AudioManager` includes `bluedroid_sbc.h` directly.
Review note: I didn’t find other functional issues in the switch code after the TODOs were filled. The one practical integration issue was the missing wrapper include path, which is now fixed.
- `rg` confirms the marked Bluedroid TODOs are gone.
- `lib\bluedroid\bluedroid_sbc.c` still compiles directly with the ESP32 Xtensa compiler.
- `pio pkg pack lib\bluedroid` succeeds, though PlatformIO still prints its local `.platformio\.cache` ownership warning.
- Full `pio run` still fails on the existing ESP32 WiFi `Network.h` include problem before a complete firmware link.
